analysis.overall_assessment: The single detected tea leaf shows multiple yellow lesions indicating severe physiological stress, likely from environmental pollutants. The leaf's compromised structure and discoloration suggest significant damage to photosynthetic capacity.

pollution_reduction_solutions: Install windbreaks with SO₂-absorbing species (e.g., poplar trees) around plantation edges
Apply calcium-based foliar sprays (0.5% calcium chloride) as immediate protective coating
Transition to low-sulfur fuel sources for nearby machinery and collaborate with local industries on emission controls

immediate_actions: Quarantine affected plant sections to prevent cross-contamination
Conduct leaf tissue analysis for sulfur accumulation levels
Suspend plucking in the affected block until source identification
processing_recommendations.sorting_strategy: Complete rejection of all leaves showing yellow lesions; no mixing with healthy harvest
processing_recommendations.processing_method: Not recommended for commercial processing due to contamination risk
quality_preservation: Isolate unaffected adjacent leaves for separate processing
Increase oxidation time during withering to reduce sulfur compounds

waste_prevention.salvageable_portions: No usable portions due to extensive lesion coverage (>30% of leaf area)
alternative_uses: Extract tannins for natural dye production
Use in biochar production for soil remediation
waste_prevention.composting_guidelines: Layer with 3:1 ratio of brown materials (dried leaves/sawdust), maintain pH 6.5-7.0 with lime amendment, and aerate weekly to accelerate decomposition of sulfur compounds
prevention_measures: Deploy real-time SO₂ sensors at field perimeter
Implement 10m buffer zones with pollution-tolerant cover crops
Rotate to SO₂-resistant cultivars (e.g., TV1, TV23)

cost_saving_opportunities: Redirect rejected leaves to value-added byproduct streams
Claim agricultural insurance for pollution-related crop damage
Partner with local industries for emission reduction subsidies
value_recovery_methods: Convert to organic fertilizer through controlled composting
Sell to research institutions for pollution impact studies

priority_actions: Conduct 72-hour air quality monitoring at the affected site
Establish emergency windbreak barriers within 1 week
Initiate soil sulfur testing and liming if pH < 5.5
recommendations.timeline: Immediate (0-7 days): Containment; Short-term (2-4 weeks): Source identification; Medium-term (3 months): Emission control implementation
monitoring_points: New leaf emergence for symptom recurrence
SO₂ concentration levels at 0.5m above canopy
Soil sulfate content and pH biweekly
